moves.name: Enter the Fray
moves.trigger:
When you enter into combat, first set the rank of each of your foes. Then, roll to determine who is in control. If you are…
  • Facing off against your foe: Roll +heart.
  • Moving into position against an unaware foe, or striking without warning: Roll +shadow.
  • Ambushed: Roll +wits.
moves.triggerPreamble: When you enter into combat, first set the rank of each of your foes. Then, roll to determine who is in control. If you are…

stats.desc: Moving into position against an unaware foe, or striking without warning

moves.strong: Take +2 momentum. You have initiative.
moves.weak:
Choose one:
  • Bolster your position: Take +2 momentum.
  • Prepare to act: Take initiative.
moves.miss: Combat begins with you at a disadvantage. Pay the Price. Your foe has initiative.
moves.notes: The outcome determines who has initiative at the start of combat. Having initiative lets you Strike rather than Clash on the first exchange — a significant advantage.

moves.name: Strike
moves.triggerShort: When you have initiative and attack.
moves.trigger: When you have initiative and attack in close quarters, roll +iron. When you have initiative and attack at range, roll +edge.
moves.triggerPreamble: When you have initiative and attack, roll…
moves.strong: Inflict double harm. You retain initiative.
moves.weak: Inflict your harm and lose initiative.
moves.miss: Your attack fails and you must Pay the Price. Your foe has initiative.
moves.notes: You must have initiative to Strike. If you don't, use Clash instead. On a strong hit, you deal your weapon's harm +1 bonus and keep initiative for follow-up attacks.

moves.name: Clash
moves.triggerShort: When your foe has initiative and you fight back.
moves.trigger: When your foe has initiative and you fight with them in close quarters, roll +iron. When you exchange a volley at range, or shoot at an advancing foe, roll +edge.
moves.triggerPreamble: When your foe has initiative and you fight back, roll…

stats.desc: Fight with them in close quarters
Exchange a volley at range, or shoot at an advancing foe

moves.strong:
Inflict your harm, you have the initiative, and choose one:
  • You bolster your position: Take +1 momentum.
  • You find an opening: Inflict double harm.
moves.weak: Inflict your harm, but then Pay the Price. Your foe has initiative.
moves.miss: You are outmatched and must Pay the Price. Your foe has initiative.
moves.notes: Clash is your response when the enemy has initiative. The key difference from Strike: you're reacting, not attacking from a position of strength. On a strong hit you seize initiative and choose a bonus. Use Face Danger instead if you're trying to dodge or endure the attack rather than counter-attack.

moves.name: Turn the Tide
moves.triggerShort: Once per fight, when you risk it all.
moves.trigger: Once per fight, when you risk it all, you may steal initiative from your foe to make a move (not a progress move). When you do, add +1 and take +1 momentum on a hit. If you fail to score a hit on that move, you must suffer a dire outcome. Pay the Price.
moves.logBody: You turn the tide and seize the initiative. Add +1 to your next move and take +1 momentum on a hit. If you fail to score a hit on that move, you must suffer a dire outcome. Pay the Price.
moves.notes: Once per fight only. This lets you steal initiative and make any non-progress move at +1. On a hit you also get +1 momentum. But if you miss, the consequences are severe — Pay the Price with a dire outcome. Use it when you're desperate.

moves.name: Battle
moves.triggerShort: When you fight a battle in a blur.
moves.trigger:
When you fight a battle, and it happens in a blur, envision your objective and roll. If you primarily…
  • Fight at range, or using your speed and the terrain to your advantage: Roll +edge.
  • Fight depending on your courage, allies, or companions: Roll +heart.
  • Fight in close to overpower your opponents: Roll +iron.
  • Fight using trickery to befuddle your opponents: Roll +shadow.
  • Fight using careful tactics to outsmart your opponents: Roll +wits.
moves.triggerPreamble: When you fight a battle, and it happens in a blur, envision your objective and roll. If you primarily…

stats.desc: Fight at range, or using your speed and the terrain to your advantage
Fight depending on your courage, allies, or companions
Fight in close to overpower your opponents
Fight using trickery to befuddle your opponents
Fight using careful tactics to outsmart your opponents

moves.strong: You achieve your objective unconditionally. Take +2 momentum.
moves.weak: You achieve your objective, but not without cost. Pay the Price.
moves.miss: You are defeated and the objective is lost to you. Pay the Price.
moves.notes: This move is used as an alternative to a detailed combat scene. When you want to zoom out and resolve a fight in a single roll, make this move.

moves.name: End the Fight
moves.triggerShort: When you score a strong hit on a decisive action and resolve the fight.
moves.trigger: When you make a move to take decisive action, and score a strong hit, you may resolve the outcome of this fight. If you do, roll the challenge dice and compare to your progress. Momentum is ignored on this roll.
moves.strong: This foe is no longer in the fight. They are killed, out of action, flee, or surrender as appropriate to the situation and your intent (Ask the Oracle if unsure).
moves.weak:
As above, but you must also choose one:
  • It's worse than you thought: Endure Harm.
  • You are overcome: Endure Stress.
  • Your victory is short-lived: A new danger or foe appears, or an existing danger worsens.
  • You suffer collateral damage: Something of value is lost or broken, or someone important must pay the cost.
  • You'll pay for it: An objective falls out of reach.
  • Others won't forget: You are marked for vengeance.
moves.miss: You have lost this fight. Pay the Price.
moves.notes: Progress move — roll challenge dice against your foe's progress track, not a stat. Momentum is ignored. You can only attempt this after scoring a strong hit on a decisive action. A miss means you've lost the fight entirely.
